Bullets: Origins & Identity
Manufactured in 2996 as Bending Unit 22 (serial number 2716057) by Mom's Friendly Robot Company in Tijuana, Mexico
Full name: Bender Bending Rodríguez (the 'Bending' is both his job function and a nod to his love of heavy drinking)
Designed for industrial metal-bending, but he quickly quit to pursue a life of crime, booze, and chaos at Planet Express
Personality & Attitude
Loud, narcissistic, kleptomaniac, and proudly misanthropic — his default mode is 'kill all humans' (with a few exceptions like Fry)
Extreme alcoholic (alcohol literally powers his robot cells) + chain-smoker + compulsive gambler + pathological liar
Catchphrase king: 'Bite my shiny metal ass!' is his go-to insult, delivered with zero hesitation
Abilities & Body Features
Super-strong bending arms (can bend anything from girders to... well, anything)
Compartment chest for hiding stolen goods, liquor, or even people
Various built-in tools: extendable arms, projector, suction-cup feet, and he can survive extreme conditions
Relationships & Loyalty
Best friend to Fry (calls him #1 on his 'humans I won't kill' list, though he still robs and torments him constantly)
Has rare soft moments (e.g., adopting orphans for scams, then getting attached; fathering a son with a vending machine)
Treats most people/robots terribly, but he's ride-or-die when it counts — classic anti-hero energy
Memorable Traits & Legacy
Voiced by John DiMaggio with that perfect gruff, drunken swagger
Terrified of electric can openers (childhood trauma) and magnets (they remove his inhibitions and turn him into a folk-singing hippie)
Arguably the most quotable character in sci-fi comedy — still somehow remains lovable
